question_title: Will ROP's market close price on 2026-02-24 be higher than its market close price on 2026-02-18?
summary: ROP is in a severe downtrend after disappointing Q4 earnings on Jan 27 (-13% that day, -27% over 6 weeks to 52-week lows). Multiple analyst downgrades followed. Monte Carlo simulations using recent empirical returns consistently estimate a 15-28% probability of a positive 4-day return (Feb 18→24). The statistically significant negative mean daily return (-1.1%, t-test p=0.047) drives this. A modest upward adjustment to ~33% accounts for small sample uncertainty, oversold conditions near the 52-week low, and the possibility the crash regime moderates 3 weeks post-earnings.

factors.description: Strong downtrend: Monte Carlo bootstrap from all recent returns gives ~22% probability of 4-day gain; parametric model also gives 22%
Accelerating decline: last 10 trading days show even worse returns (bootstrap P=14.5%), mean daily return -1.7%
Multiple analyst downgrades in January (Mizuho to Underperform, Argus to Hold, multiple price target cuts)
Fundamental headwinds persist: weak Deltek (govt contractor), freight market recession at DAT, 2026 guidance below consensus
Oversold at 52-week low: stock at $316 vs 52-week high of $595, potential for mean-reversion bounce
Insider buying signal: CEO bought shares at $358 on Feb 6, some value investors expressing interest
Small sample uncertainty: only 29 daily returns in estimation window, bootstrap estimates have wide confidence intervals
Unconditional base rate: stocks go up ~52% of the time over any 4-day window, anchoring pull toward 50%

reasoning: Now let me run a Monte Carlo simulation using the empirical data.Let me check if there's any news explaining this massive decline.
